criteria.criterion: A committed store compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) is created at init at a fixed path, written atomically (tempfile + rename), and read by a gate function that raises StoreIncompatibleError; the module exports KNOWN_CAPABILITIES: frozenset[str].

An absent record (pre-v1.0 store) is treated as legacy format version 0 / compatible (pass-through, not blocked), and the ensure unit idempotently writes the record on the next init / fsck-repair / MCP-boot — verified by a test that opens a record-less store with a v1.0 build, confirms it operates, and confirms the record is then present; the test also confirms a missed ensure sweep does not block writes.

The gate is inserted inside lock.acquire() (src/rebar/_store/lock.py, after path resolution, before fcntl/mkdir) gating every acquisition unconditionally; plus explicit check_store_compat() at the top of fsck_recover_cli and in reconcile._apply_mutations() as 'if persist: check_store_compat(...)'.

A corrupt/unreadable record (truncated JSON / parse error) is treated as fail-closed (NOT as absent) with a diagnostic naming the parse error and path — verified by a test writing a truncated record and asserting a mutating entry point is blocked.

A rollback test simulates a pre-v1.0 binary by monkeypatching check_store_compat to a no-op against a store carrying the committed .store-compat.json, and asserts operable + normal write succeeds + .store-compat.json is byte-unchanged afterward. The ignored-unknown guarantee and delete compensating path are documented in docs/migrations.md.

Under an incompatible/corrupt record, read-only commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) exit 0; fsck diagnostic JSON carries compat_error object with kind/detail; same message on stderr WARNING; fsck repair mode exits non-zero — verified by test asserting .compat_error.kind via exact value check and repair-mode non-zero exit.

The gate is un-swallowable on the ensure-sweep path: run_ensures has an explicit 'except StoreIncompatibleError: raise' before its broad except Exception, so init/MCP-boot/fsck-repair against an incompatible store fail closed rather than logging a sweep no-op.

Optional additive (unknown-but-not-required) events retain preserve-and-ignore behaviour — existing forward-compat contract tests still pass, and a new test proves a required capability fails closed while an optional additive event does not.
docs/migrations.md documents the record, the four states, the fail-closed gate, the deployment-order rationale (why the reader ships in v1.0), and the concrete rollback state.

citation.description: Ticket comment attestation: change 703 (Change-Id I01f85f87) LANDED on main via Gerrit — status MERGED — with LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 (GitHub Actions CI: build/test/lint/typecheck/module-size all green). Local pre-flight: make lint + make typecheck clean; full make test 5960 passed.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test pass, and the change lands via the Gerrit two-vote gate.
